footer.statusDescription: Best fit for work that needs careful implementation and a setup that stays manageable after launch.
footer.summary: I build public-facing websites, dashboards, and internal tools with the same focus on clarity and day-to-day usability.
footer.builtWith: Built and maintained with a practical product mindset.

hero.availability: Available for a small number of client projects
hero.headline: I build websites and dashboards
hero.highlight: that hold up in real use.
hero.subheadline: I work with small teams and founders who need a public site, an admin area, or a product workflow that is clear to run and easier to maintain.
hero.surfaceValue: Public sites, dashboards, and internal tools
hero.deliveryValue: Clear scope, steady delivery, and practical decisions
hero.languageValue: Arabic and English from the start
hero.cardDescription: What matters to me is not only how the interface looks, but how content gets managed, how data gets handled, and how easy the product is to change after launch.
hero.outcomesText: A clearer public experience, an admin flow that makes sense, and code that is easier to update later.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: I build the parts people use and the parts teams manage.
about.intro: Most of the work I enjoy sits between a public-facing product and the internal systems behind it. I like projects that need clear structure, sensible trade-offs, and fewer headaches after launch.
about.strength1: Interfaces that make the important actions obvious
about.strength2: Dashboards and data flows that stay manageable over time
about.strength3: Technical decisions that make later changes easier
about.editorComment: A short working note, not the whole picture.

projects.title: Selected
projects.subtitle: A mix of public websites, dashboards, and product workflows built for real use, not just presentation.
projects.search: Search by project, type, or technology...
projects.noProjectsDescription: Try a different term or clear the filters to see everything.

skills.title: What I use in
skills.subtitle: These are the languages, frameworks, and tools I tend to rely on when a project needs to stay clear, maintainable, and easy to extend.
skills.noSkills: Skills will be listed here soon.

blog.title: Notes on
blog.subtitle: Short notes on building public websites, dashboards, internal tools, and the decisions behind them.
blog.search: Search by title, category, or topic...
blog.noArticlesDescription: Try another term or switch the active topic.

services.title: How I can help
services.description: Most of my work sits across three areas: shaping the public experience, building the internal workflow behind it, and helping teams make practical technical decisions.
services.frontendDesc: Marketing sites, company websites, and product-facing pages that stay clear, fast, and easy to edit.
services.backendDesc: APIs, data models, and operational logic that support the product without turning into a maintenance problem.
services.mobileDesc: Admin areas and internal flows that make day-to-day work easier for the team using them.
services.consultingDesc: Scoping, architecture direction, reviews, and implementation decisions when a project needs a practical path forward.

contact.title: If you have a project in mind,
contact.subtitle: The most helpful first message is a simple one: what you are building, where things stand, and where you need help. If I am a fit, I will reply with a practical next step.
contact.name: Name
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell me what you are building, what stage you are in, and what kind of help you need.
contact.signInPrefill: Sign in if you want your details filled in automatically.
contact.successMsg: Thanks for the context. I will read it and get back to you as soon as I can.
contact.whatsappReqGuest: Please include a direct contact method if you are sending as a guest.
contact.availableDesc: I am most useful on projects that need a clear public experience and the systems behind it to work well too.
contact.remote: Cairo, Egypt / Working remotely with clients
contact.workingHoursQ: When should I expect a reply?
contact.workingHoursA: Usually within 1 to 2 business days, depending on scope and current workload.
contact.agenciesQ: Do you work with agencies and teams?
contact.agenciesA: Yes. I work with founders, small teams, and agencies when the project needs hands-on delivery.

validation.name: Name must be at least 2 characters.
validation.email: Please enter a valid email address.
validation.message: Message must be at least 10 characters.

login.unauthorized: Unauthorized access: This dashboard is restricted to the administrator.
login.desc: Authenticated access required to manage site content, articles, and dashboard configurations.
login.signInToAccess: Sign in to access your dashboard.
login.accessRestricted: Access is strictly restricted to the site owner.

dashboardOverview.welcomeBack: A live snapshot of the content and admin surfaces in this workspace.
dashboardOverview.featuredProjectsHint: Curated projects flagged for the public showcase.
dashboardOverview.blogPostsHint: Articles available for the public blog surface.
dashboardOverview.unreadMessagesHint: Messages waiting for review or follow-up.
dashboardOverview.recentActivityHint: Latest updates from projects, blog content, and inbound contact messages.
dashboardOverview.emptyDescription: Create or update content from the dashboard and it will appear here.
dashboardOverview.workspaceSnapshotHint: A quick summary of the latest items across the collections you manage.
dashboardOverview.quickActionsEmptyDescription: Enable at least one module in Dashboard Settings to keep your shortcuts here.

dashboardControl.title: Dashboard Settings
dashboardControl.description: Control the dashboard shell itself: branding, intro copy, sidebar order, overview widgets, and quick actions.
dashboardControl.brandingHint: These values shape how the internal workspace introduces itself before the admin reaches any module.
dashboardControl.subtitle: Sidebar Subtitle
dashboardControl.sidebarHint: Reorder modules, change their labels, and hide the ones you do not want in the admin shell.
dashboardControl.label: Module Label
dashboardControl.visualPresetHint: Choose the accent and surface treatment used by the internal dashboard shell.
dashboardControl.overviewWidgetsHint: Pick which dashboard widgets appear on the Control Center and how they are ordered.
dashboardControl.quickActionsHint: Keep the highest-priority modules one click away from the top of the overview screen.
dashboardControl.previewHint: A quick shell snapshot showing how the sidebar and Control Center will feel after saving.

dashboardPages.title: Page Composer
dashboardPages.description: Manage public-page structure, variants, and section copy through safe config documents instead of hardcoded layouts.
dashboardPages.selectPageHint: Choose the public route you want to manage before editing its metadata and sections.
dashboardPages.pageIdentityHint: These values define the page-level label, route slug, and publish state for the selected route.
dashboardPages.seoOverridesHint: Use these overrides when the page needs a title, description, or share image that differs from the global SEO baseline.
dashboardPages.pageOverviewHint: A quick summary of the selected route and the current section configuration.
dashboardPages.sectionsHint: Reorder sections, switch variants, and edit structured copy without leaving the admin workspace.
dashboardPages.saveError: Failed to save the page composition.

dashboardSite.title: Site Settings
dashboardSite.description: Manage the public identity, bilingual labels, and brand basics that define how the site appears before visitors reach the content.
dashboardSite.profileImageCropHint: Control how the profile photo is cropped in the About section without editing the original image.
dashboardSite.primaryCtaEnabledHint: Use this switch if the public shell should always show a single primary action.
dashboardSite.brandGuidanceBody: Keep the site name concise, the tagline specific, and the CTA action-oriented. These values appear across the public shell and should describe the product surface clearly without sounding generic.
dashboardSite.profileMirrorWarningTitle: The site brand currently mirrors the profile name.
dashboardSite.profileMirrorWarningBody: This makes the public navbar and footer show the personal profile name instead of the intended site brand. Restore the canonical site brand, then save the changes.

dashboardTheme.title: Theme Settings
dashboardTheme.description: Control the public token set for accent color, surfaces, radii, shadows, and background mood without exposing raw CSS editing.
dashboardTheme.previewCardDescription: A compact view of the current palette, surface treatment, and action emphasis.
dashboardTheme.previewTitle: Token-safe styling with a strong visual direction
dashboardTheme.previewBody: These settings update the public shell through constrained design tokens so the site stays cohesive, legible, and easy to maintain.
dashboardTheme.notesBody: Choose one accent family and one surface style that match the brand. Use radius and shadow as supporting signals, not as the main personality of the interface.

dashboardNavigation.title: Navigation Settings
dashboardNavigation.description: Manage the public navigation structure, localized labels, and top-level header controls from one place.
dashboardNavigation.label: Label
dashboardNavigation.showLanguageToggleHint: Display the public language switcher in the top navigation.
dashboardNavigation.showThemeToggleHint: Allow visitors to toggle between light and dark only when the public theme mode follows the system.

dashboardFooter.title: Footer Settings
dashboardFooter.description: Control footer messaging, status-strip copy, quick links, and social/contact surfaces shown at the bottom of the public site.

dashboardContact.title: Contact Settings
dashboardContact.description: Set the direct contact channels, availability copy, location wording, and response-time messaging used across the public contact experience.

dashboardSeo.title: SEO Settings
dashboardSeo.description: Manage global metadata defaults that support the public shell, page-level SEO fallbacks, and Open Graph basics.
dashboardSeo.profileMirrorWarningTitle: The SEO title currently mirrors the profile name.
dashboardSeo.profileMirrorWarningBody: This makes the browser title fall back to the personal name instead of the branded site title. Restore the canonical SEO title, then save the changes.

dashboardProjects.managePortfolio: Manage the projects shown across the public portfolio and detail pages.
dashboardProjects.title: Project Title
dashboardProjects.slugHint: Short URL-friendly name such as portfolio-dashboard.
dashboardProjects.galleryHint: One image URL per line, or separate multiple URLs with commas.
dashboardProjects.galleryHelper: Gallery images appear on the project detail page in the same order shown here.
dashboardProjects.noProjectsDescription: Create the first project to populate both the public portfolio and the featured showcase.
dashboardProjects.confirmDelete: This will permanently remove the project from the dashboard and public site.

dashboardSkills.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this skill?
dashboardSkills.manageProficiencies: Manage technical skills, tools, and proficiencies.
dashboardSkills.noSkills: No skills added yet. Click 'Add Skill' to begin.
dashboardSkills.emptyDescription: Add the first skill to power the public skills page.

dashboardBlog.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this article?
dashboardBlog.publishThoughts: Publish your thoughts and articles to the world.
dashboardBlog.title: Title
dashboardBlog.featuredHint: Show this article in featured blog surfaces.
dashboardBlog.emptyDescription: Publish the first article to populate the public blog page.

dashboardTestimonials.title: Testimonials
dashboardTestimonials.description: Manage social proof quotes, author details, ordering, and public visibility.
dashboardTestimonials.name: Name
dashboardTestimonials.orderHintBody: Lower values appear first. Featured visible items are prioritized before the rest on the public site.
dashboardTestimonials.emptyDescription: Add the first testimonial to power the public testimonials section.
dashboardTestimonials.deleteDescription: This will permanently remove the testimonial from the dashboard and public site.

dashboardMessages.emptyInbox: No messages yet. Your inbox is empty.
dashboardMessages.deleteDesc: This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete the message.

dashboardSettings.manageGlobal: Update the public profile, bilingual copy, meta content, and visual assets used across the site.
dashboardSettings.showRecruiters: Show that you are currently open to new work.

testimonials.title: Selected Feedback
testimonials.subtitle: Short comments that reflect delivery quality and implementation depth.
testimonials.quote1: Clear communication, strong execution, and a much better product surface than where we started.
testimonials.quote2: The project moved from disconnected screens to a coherent product workflow with better performance and cleaner implementation.

cta.description: Share the goal, where things stand, and where you need help. If it fits, I will reply with a practical next step.

featuredProjects.title: A few projects that show how I work.
featuredProjects.subtitle: Public sites, dashboards, and product flows where structure mattered as much as visuals.
featuredProjects.name: name
featuredProjects.emptyDescription: Feature a few projects from the dashboard and they will appear here.

blogPost.connectDescription: If you need a public site, dashboard, or internal flow in the same spirit, send the context and I can tell you if I am a fit.

ctaSection.title: Start with the real project context.
ctaSection.description: A short message with the goal, current stage, and constraints is usually enough for a useful first reply.
ctaSection.responseDescription: The first reply usually covers fit, rough scope, and the clearest next step.
ctaSection.focusValue: Public sites, dashboards, and internal tools
ctaSection.focusDescription: Most useful when the work needs both a clear front end and a practical system behind it.

showcase.eyebrow: How the work is set up
showcase.title: Not just a front layer.
showcase.subtitle: The public pages, the admin area, and the content system are designed to support the same product, so the site stays useful after launch.
showcase.publicDescription: Project pages, writing, and contact flows shaped for people seeing the work for the first time.
showcase.adminDescription: The place where content, links, and core settings can be updated without friction.
showcase.crudDescription: Real data-backed sections instead of static placeholders or one-off demos.
showcase.i18nDescription: Both languages are treated as part of the product, including RTL support across the public site.

projectDetail.notFoundDescription: The requested project is not available in the current workspace data.
projectDetail.caseStudyFallback: This project is published and linked, but its structured case-study summary has not been filled in yet from the dashboard.
projectDetail.noTech: No technologies were listed for this project yet.

dashboardMedia.title: Media Library
dashboardMedia.description: Manage reusable images and videos linked across projects, blog posts, skills, and testimonials.
dashboardMedia.previewHint: Preview updates automatically as soon as the asset URL is valid.
dashboardMedia.emptyDescription: Add the first asset to reuse it across projects, skills, blog posts, and testimonials.
dashboardMedia.deleteDescription: This will permanently remove the asset from the media library.

dashboardMediaPicker.description: Pick an asset from the library, upload a compressed image from this device, or keep a direct URL override.
dashboardMediaPicker.uploadHint: Images are converted to WebP, resized to 1600px max, and stored in Firestore under 300 KB.
dashboardMediaPicker.imageTooLarge: This image is still too large after compression. Try a smaller file.
